CRYOSTATS
# Cryostats that support your work from first experiments to scaled quantum systems
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/products/cryostats/#characterization)
  
CRYOSTATS
## Cryostats that support your work from first experiments to scaled quantum systems
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/products/cryostats/#characterization)
## L-Type Rapid
### Super-fast characterization cryostat for sub-kelvin and kelvin temperatures 
###### Speed up your development cycles with the fastest cryostat on the market. 
###### Empower your team with a fully automatic, cryogen free system.
###### Take advantage of the versatility of a modular platform by configuring the device for your specific use case.   

###### 50 mK to room temperature. 
[Read more](https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat)
![Front view of a L-Type Rapid cryostat](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/L-TYPE_FRONT_723x1376v5-538x1024.png)
## X-Type
### The cooling architecture for scalable quantum systems
###### Scalable cooling infrastructure – Increase cooling power on demand by adding cooling modules
###### Expandable system architecture – Grow system capacity to support larger and more
###### Helium-3-free
[Read more](https://kiutra.com/products/x-type/)
## Cryostats for specialized use cases
### Cryogenic environments for the testing and operation of quantum devices and integrated setups
#####  **S-Type**  
Rack-integrated sub-kelvin platform for continuous cooling without helium-3
#####  **T-Type Optical**  
Optical kelvin temperature cryostat optimized for trapped-ion chips 
[Read More](https://kiutra.com/products/cryostats-for-specialized-use-cases/)

	$(window).on('load', () => {
		var lastScrollTop = 0, delta = 20;
		$(window).scroll(function(){
			// Header Visibilty
			var nowScrollTop = $(this).scrollTop();
			if(Math.abs(lastScrollTop - nowScrollTop) >= delta){
			if (nowScrollTop > lastScrollTop){ 
		 		$("header").addClass("invisible")
		 	} else {
		 		$("header").removeClass("invisible") 
			}
			if (nowScrollTop <= 0) {
				$("header").removeClass("invisible")
				lastScrollTop = 0;
			} else {
				lastScrollTop = nowScrollTop;
			}
			}
		});
	});	
</script>
